error_signature: One bad record crashes the whole workflow
title: Single bad item causes workflow failure
symptom: When processing a batch of items, if one item is malformed, the entire workflow fails, preventing valid items from being processed.
not_this_if: Using a different error handling strategy
root_cause: In n8n, most nodes fail the entire execution when an error is thrown, which means that a single malformed item can halt the processing of all subsequent valid items, leading to incomplete executions and the need for complex deduplication logic on retries.
fix_steps: Modify the Function node to catch errors for each item instead of throwing them.
Return a status for each item indicating success or failure, allowing valid items to continue processing.
Route failed items to a dead-letter table for later review.
Ensure all database writes are idempotent to avoid duplicates on retries.
agent_instruction: Implement error handling in the Function node to allow valid items to be processed even if some are malformed.
